title: Ceviche de Bacalao con Maripaha
description: A savory and slightly sour Uruguayan dish featuring cod that has been marinated in a flavorful vegetable-based sauce with crisp vegetables for an adventurous twist, vegetarian-friendly.

ingredients.name: Canned white fish (cod)
ingredients.notes: Choose a firm white fish like cod for best results.

ingredients.name: Fresh vegetables blend (tomato, onion, cilantro, lime leaves)
ingredients.notes: Includes fresh tomatoes, finely chopped onions, fresh cilantro, and whole lime leaves.

ingredients.name: Canned chickpeas
ingredients.notes: Use for added protein and texture.

ingredients.name: Sour cream (vegetarian)
ingredients.notes: Regular sour cream can be used if vegetarian is unavailable.

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing vegetables and adding flavor to the fish.

ingredients.name: Vegetable stock (optional)
ingredients.notes: To reduce sodium in the dish if desired.

instructions: Sauté onion and cilantro until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add vegetable oil and sauté for an additional minute.
Toss in canned white fish pieces and cook over medium heat until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Stir in lime juice, salt, and vegetable stock. Reduce by half if using regular sour cream or to taste with fresh lime juice.
Add can of chickpeas and cooked vegetables mix into the white fish mixture.
Mix in fresh vegetable blend (tomato, onion, cilantro) until well combined.
Simmer for another 10-15 minutes until vegetables are tender and flavors are melded together.
Serve immediately as an appetizer or main course.
